actions.label: See why my build broke
actions.desc: Shows your latest automated checks (CI runs) with pass/fail status, newest first

actions.label: Read the full error log for a failed run
actions.desc: Opens the detailed log for one run so you can see exactly what failed
actions.input_prompt: Which run? Paste the run ID from the build list (the long number)

actions.label: Check on my pull requests
actions.desc: Lists all open pull requests (proposed changes) with their numbers and titles

actions.label: Look at one pull request in detail
actions.desc: Shows the title, description, checks, and review status for one pull request
actions.input_prompt: Which pull request? Enter its number (e.g. 2295)

actions.label: Save and send my work to GitHub
actions.command: git add -A && git commit -m "{input}" && git push
actions.desc: Saves all your changes with a short note and uploads them to GitHub
actions.input_prompt: In a few words, what did you change? (this becomes the save note)

actions.label: Open a pull request for my changes
actions.desc: Proposes your pushed changes for review and merging, using your last save note as the title

actions.label: Merge a pull request into the project
actions.desc: Combines an approved pull request into the main project (squashed into one tidy change)
actions.input_prompt: Which pull request should I merge in? Enter its number

actions.label: Turn my writing into secret tokens
actions.input_prompt: What text do you want to turn into secret tokens?
actions.desc: Encodes your text into Kor'aelin sacred-tongue tokens

actions.label: Read my secret tokens back as plain text
actions.input_prompt: Paste the Kor'aelin tokens to turn back into text
actions.desc: Decodes Kor'aelin tokens back into the original words

actions.label: Hide my writing in the veil tongue
actions.input_prompt: What text do you want to hide in the Umbroth veil tongue?
actions.desc: Encodes your text into Umbroth (the redaction/veil tongue)

actions.label: Lock my writing with the binding tongue
actions.input_prompt: What text do you want to bind in the Runethic tongue?
actions.desc: Encodes your text into Runethic (the salt/binding tongue)

actions.label: See all six sacred tongues I can use
actions.desc: Lists the 6 sacred tongues with samples and what each is for

actions.label: Turn my writing into tokens (any tongue)
actions.input_prompt: Type a tongue code (ko, av, ru, ca, um, dr) then your text
actions.desc: Encodes text into the tongue you pick (ko/av/ru/ca/um/dr)

actions.label: Turn my words into atoms
actions.input_prompt: What text should I turn into atomic states?
actions.desc: Maps each word in your text to an atomic token state (elements + 6-tongue charges)

actions.label: Translate an SCBE action to chemistry
actions.input_prompt: Which SCBE operation? (e.g. release, bind, compare)
actions.desc: Shows the chemistry analogue (like dissociation or bonding) for an SCBE operation

actions.label: Clean up a molecule formula
actions.input_prompt: Paste a SMILES molecule string (e.g. CCO for ethanol)
actions.desc: Converts a SMILES molecule into its clean canonical form via RDKit/Open Babel

actions.label: Check how strongly six tongues bond
actions.desc: Scores the 6 Sacred Tongue coordinates as molecule-like bonds and reports stability

actions.label: Show the orbital shell map
actions.desc: Prints the GeoSeed golden-ratio orbital shells for all six tongues (s/p/d/f/g/h)

actions.label: Check the chemistry engine is healthy
actions.desc: Fast inventory of the chemistry capability files and runtime probes with pass rate

actions.label: Is this text risky? Allow or block
actions.input_prompt: Paste the text you want checked for risk
actions.desc: Scores your text and tells you ALLOW (safe) or DENY (risky)

actions.label: Pass/fail safety check on this text
actions.input_prompt: Paste the text to run through the safety gate
actions.desc: Runs the gate that flags a failure if the text is blocked

actions.label: See the full safety breakdown for this text
actions.input_prompt: Paste the text you want a detailed safety report on
actions.desc: Runs the full 14-layer governance check and shows every score

actions.label: Get the safety result as data (JSON)
actions.input_prompt: Paste the text to score and get JSON back
actions.desc: Same risk check but returns machine-readable JSON details

actions.label: Check one of my files for problems
actions.input_prompt: Which file? (path to the file, e.g. scbe.py)
actions.desc: Lints and reviews a code file and lists any issues found

actions.label: Ask the AI anything
actions.input_prompt: What do you want to ask the AI?
actions.desc: Type a question in plain English and get a straight answer from the AI.

actions.label: Have the AI do a task for me
actions.input_prompt: What should the AI do? (describe the task)
actions.desc: Tell the AI a task and let it work on it for you (can change files).

actions.label: Check my code file for problems
actions.input_prompt: Which file? (paste the full path to your code file)
actions.desc: Scans a code file for errors and red flags, and tells you if it's broken.

actions.label: Get a plain review of my code
actions.input_prompt: Which file? (paste the full path to your code file)
actions.desc: Reads your code file and gives friendly suggestions on what to improve.

actions.label: Full health check on my code
actions.input_prompt: Which file? (paste the full path to your code file)
actions.desc: Runs both the problem scan and the review in one go for a full report.

actions.label: Translate code into another language
actions.input_prompt: Which language? (e.g. rust, javascript, python, go, java)
actions.desc: Turns a small program into the programming language you pick.

actions.label: Show all languages I can translate to
actions.desc: Lists every programming language and operation the translator supports.

actions.label: Explain a part of the safety system
actions.input_prompt: Which layer or idea? (e.g. L13, harmonic, breathing)
actions.desc: Explains a pipeline layer or concept in simple terms.

actions.label: Find one of my notes by name
actions.input_prompt: What word should be in the note's name?
actions.desc: Searches all your vaults for notes/docs whose filename matches your text

actions.label: Search inside my notes for a word
actions.input_prompt: What word or phrase should I look for inside your notes?
actions.desc: Looks inside the text of your notes, not just the filenames

actions.label: Open the newest note about something
actions.input_prompt: What is the note about? (a word from its name)
actions.desc: Finds the newest matching note and opens it in your default app

actions.label: Show what I worked on lately
actions.desc: Lists your most recently dated notes so you can pick up where you left off

actions.label: See all my note vaults
actions.desc: Lists your real Obsidian vaults and how many notes each holds

actions.label: Open one of my vault folders
actions.input_prompt: Which vault? (a word from its folder name, e.g. Avalon)
actions.desc: Opens the matching vault folder in File Explorer

actions.label: Check if my PC is healthy
actions.desc: Checks your RAM, free disk space, and warns if anything is running low

actions.label: Make sure SCBE itself is working
actions.desc: Runs a quick self-test of all the core parts and tells you if everything passed

actions.label: See what SCBE knows about my project
actions.desc: Shows the project version, languages, tongues, and pipeline layers at a glance

actions.label: Check my tools are set up right
actions.desc: Checks that helper tools like git, node, and gh are installed and shows what's missing

actions.label: Get a clear warning if storage is low
actions.desc: Runs the PC health check and flags low disk space sooner so you get more warning

actions.label: Save a health report I can keep
actions.desc: Runs the health check and prints a full report you can save or share

actions.label: See, hear & feel my words
actions.input_prompt: Type any words you want to experience across the senses:
actions.desc: Renders your text as a sound wave, a musical note, a texture, a taste, and a flow — all five senses at once.

actions.label: Show me a quick taste of the senses
actions.desc: A ready-made example so you can instantly see what the synesthesia view looks like.

actions.label: What does my writing taste like?
actions.desc: Runs a warm example phrase to show the taste and feel readouts (clean, smooth, calm).

actions.label: Explain what the Risk layer does
actions.desc: Plain explanation of L13, the layer that decides Allow / Quarantine / Escalate / Deny.

actions.label: Explain what the Sound layer does
actions.desc: Plain explanation of L14, the audio axis that turns text into tones and cymatic sound.

actions.label: Explain a layer by its number
actions.input_prompt: Which layer? Type a number L1 through L14 (for example L12):
actions.desc: Tells you in plain words what any of the 14 layers (L1-L14) actually does.
